summary: Markdown-first knowledge system: Obsidian is the creator workspace; Vault/Lore is the only canonical public source; Node build scripts validate and compile Lore into generated Astro/Starlight content and datasets; the fictional calendar/timeline core is shared by the website and a maintained local Obsidian plugin.

source_of_truth.creator_entities: Markdown/YAML notes in Vault/Drafts/Databases or Vault/Lore

zones.label: Obsidian authoring
zones.notes: Bases are views. Markdown/YAML notes remain authoritative.

zones.label: Private StoryLine workspace
zones.notes: Hard private boundary. Never scanned as public canon. VISCERIUM Timelines reads scenes in memory only.

zones.label: Canonical Lore boundary
zones.notes: Only notes under Lore with status:published may publish. Routes derive from relative paths.

zones.label: Content/build pipeline
zones.notes: Site/scripts/build-content.mjs is the orchestration choke point for sync/dev/build.

zones.label: Shared chronology core
zones.notes: Shared by Node/Astro and the local Obsidian plugin. Do not duplicate calendar arithmetic in UI/generators.

zones.label: Astro/Starlight web runtime
zones.notes: Static site. Private server secrets are intentionally excluded.

zones.label: VISCERIUM Timelines Obsidian runtime
zones.notes: Tools is the implementation source. The Vault plugin bundle is generated/synchronized by CI.

zones.label: Tests and CI
zones.notes: Node 24 is the supported build/CI runtime.

zones.label: Static deployment
zones.notes: Root Site; build npm run build; output dist; SITE_URL=https://codex.viscerium.co.uk; no required committed Wrangler file.

story_entity_authoring: Vault/Home.md quick action or Templater command
run cd Site && npm run doctor:vault after broad structural edits
human promotion to Vault/Lore only when genuinely canonical/public
public_build: enforce WebP/SVG asset policy using validate-vault-assets.mjs
rewrite Obsidian wikilinks/embeds and copy referenced assets
transform timeline/Chronos shortcodes and generate category pages
validate generated docs in build mode
generate Site/src/data/maps.json from generated public metadata
canonical_timeline: Site/src/lib/calendar/runtime.mjs converts source date to absolute world-day
Site/src/lib/timeline/compiler.mjs validates chronology and era membership
Chronos adapter maps absolute days to presentation coordinates and metadata
TimelineApp.astro + TimelineIsland.tsx provide static fallback + client interaction
native_chronos: author fenced chronos block in a note
Chronos community plugin renders it in Obsidian
site sync transforms it to ChronosEmbed for Starlight
it remains note-local presentation data and is never imported into canonical chronology
storyline_timeline: storyDate is converted in memory through the shared calendar model
no calendarDate, shortcode or generated canonical file is written
ci: install Chromium and run Playwright against preview
on successful push to main only: rebuild/copy local plugin bundle and bot-commit if changed

invariants: Only Vault/Lore may become public Codex content.
Never manually edit Site/src/content/docs; it is emptied/regenerated.
Never author canonical events/maps in generated Site/src/data JSON.
StoryLine storyDate and canonical calendarDate are separate sources for separate scopes.
calendarDate is the sole canonical event start date; legacy timeline.id/year/date are invalid.
Obsidian Bases are views over Markdown/YAML, not the database of record.
Public routes derive from Vault/Lore relative paths; do not author slug frontmatter.
Raster repository artwork must be WebP; genuine vector SVG is allowed and Vault SVG is safety-checked.
Tools/obsidian-viscerium-timelines is the plugin implementation source of truth; the tracked Vault bundle is generated.
Shared schema additions must pass the documented admission test and stay synchronized across templates/Bases/validators/public schema where relevant.
The current public Codex is static; do not put private server secrets into client code.

usually_touch: content.config.ts only if public output requires field

never: fork timeline compiler with calendar-specific arithmetic
change canonical source schema for a purely visual request
assume device-local Templater permissions are Git-controlled
commit secrets into static client code

site.npm run dev: build development content + Astro dev server
site.npm run build: production content pipeline + Astro build + icon validation
site.npm test: Vault Doctor + unit tests + production build

before_editing: Classify the feature into Authoring, Canon schema, Pipeline, Shared chronology, Web UI, Obsidian plugin or Deployment.
Start from an authoritative source, never a generated artifact.
Read the nearest SOP/README and existing tests.
Check whether the change crosses the Lore/Stories privacy boundary or creates a second source of truth.
during_editing: Keep source facts in Markdown/YAML and projections generated.
Reuse shared calendar/timeline libraries rather than duplicating domain logic.
Treat schema fields as maintenance liabilities; add only fields that pass the admission test.
For plugin changes, edit Tools source first and rebuild the runtime bundle.
before_handoff: Run the narrowest relevant tests first.
Run cd Site && npm run doctor:vault for creator/schema changes.
Run cd Site && npm test for normal confidence.
Run npm run benchmark:timelines for timeline compiler/performance changes.
Build the Obsidian plugin when shared timeline/StoryLine/plugin code changes.
State which generated files were regenerated and which authoritative files changed.
